root_text.an10.220:1.1: Atha kho aññataro brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an10.220:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an10.220:1.3: “ko nu kho, bho gotama, hetu ko paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjantī”ti?
root_text.an10.220:1.4: “Adhammacariyāvisamacariyāhetu kho, brāhmaṇa, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
root_text.an10.220:2.1: “Ko pana, bho gotama, hetu ko paccayo yena m’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti?
root_text.an10.220:2.2: “Dhammacariyāsamacariyāhetu kho, brāhmaṇa, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
root_text.an10.220:3.1: “Na kho ahaṁ imassa bhoto gotamassa saṅkhittena bhāsitassa vitthārena atthaṁ ājānāmi.
root_text.an10.220:3.2: Sādhu me bhavaṁ gotamo tathā dhammaṁ desetu yathāhaṁ imassa bhoto gotamassa saṅkhittena bhāsitassa vitthārena atthaṁ ājāneyyan”ti.
root_text.an10.220:3.3: “Tena hi, brāhmaṇa, suṇāhi, sādhukaṁ manasi karohi; bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an10.220:3.4: “Evaṁ, bho”ti kho so brāhmaṇo bhagavato paccassosi.
root_text.an10.220:5.1: Kathañca, brāhmaṇa, tividhā kāyena adhammacariyāvisamacariyā hoti …pe…
root_text.an10.220:5.2: evaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, tividhā kāyena adhammacariyā visamacariyā hoti.
root_text.an10.220:6.1: Kathañca, brāhmaṇa, catubbidhā vācāya adhammacariyāvisamacariyā hoti …pe…
root_text.an10.220:6.2: evaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, catubbidhā vācāya adhammacariyāvisamacariyā hoti.
root_text.an10.220:7.1: Kathañca, brāhmaṇa, tividhā manasā adhammacariyāvisamacariyā hoti …pe…
root_text.an10.220:7.2: evaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, tividhā manasā adhammacariyāvisamacariyā hoti.
root_text.an10.220:7.3: Evaṁ adhammacariyāvisamacariyāhetu kho, brāhmaṇa, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjanti.
root_text.an10.220:9.1: Kathañca, brāhmaṇa, tividhā kāyena dhammacariyāsamacariyā hoti …pe…
root_text.an10.220:9.2: evaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, tividhā kāyena dhammacariyāsamacariyā hoti.
root_text.an10.220:10.1: Kathañca, brāhmaṇa, catubbidhā vācāya dhammacariyāsamacariyā hoti …pe…
root_text.an10.220:10.2: evaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, catubbidhā vācāya dhammacariyāsamacariyā hoti.
root_text.an10.220:11.1: Kathañca, brāhmaṇa, tividhā manasā dhammacariyāsamacariyā hoti …pe…
root_text.an10.220:11.2: evaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, tividhā manasā dhammacariyāsamacariyā hoti.
root_text.an10.220:11.3: Evaṁ dhammacariyāsamacariyāhetu kho, brāhmaṇa, evam’idhekacce sattā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjantī”ti.
root_text.an10.220:12.2: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.an10.220:1.1: Then a certain brahmin went up to the Buddha and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an10.220:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an10.220:1.3: “What is the cause, worthy Gotama, what is the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell?”
translation_text.an10.220:1.4: “Unprincipled and immoral conduct is the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.”
translation_text.an10.220:2.1: “But what is the cause, worthy Gotama, what is the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm?”
translation_text.an10.220:2.2: “Principled and moral conduct is the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”
translation_text.an10.220:3.1: “I don’t understand the detailed meaning of what the worthy Gotama has said in brief.
translation_text.an10.220:3.2: Please, worthy Gotama, teach me this matter so I can understand the detailed meaning.”
translation_text.an10.220:3.3: “Well then, brahmin, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an10.220:4.1: “Brahmin, unprincipled and immoral conduct is threefold by way of body,
translation_text.an10.220:4.3: and threefold by way of mind.
translation_text.an10.220:5.1: And how is unprincipled and immoral conduct threefold by way of body? …
translation_text.an10.220:5.2: That’s how unprincipled and immoral conduct is threefold by way of body.
translation_text.an10.220:6.1: And how is unprincipled and immoral conduct fourfold by way of speech? …
translation_text.an10.220:6.2: That’s how unprincipled and immoral conduct is fourfold by way of speech.
translation_text.an10.220:7.1: And how is unprincipled and immoral conduct threefold by way of mind? …
translation_text.an10.220:7.2: That’s how unprincipled and immoral conduct is threefold by way of mind.
translation_text.an10.220:7.3: That’s how unprincipled and immoral conduct is the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.an10.220:8.1: Principled and moral conduct is threefold by way of body,
translation_text.an10.220:8.3: and threefold by way of mind.
translation_text.an10.220:9.1: And how is principled and moral conduct threefold by way of body? …
translation_text.an10.220:9.2: That’s how principled and moral conduct is threefold by way of body.
translation_text.an10.220:10.1: And how is principled and moral conduct fourfold by way of speech? …
translation_text.an10.220:10.2: That’s how principled and moral conduct is fourfold by way of speech.
translation_text.an10.220:11.1: And how is principled and moral conduct threefold by way of mind? …
translation_text.an10.220:11.2: That’s how principled and moral conduct is threefold by way of mind.
translation_text.an10.220:11.3: That’s how principled and moral conduct is the reason why some sentient beings, when their body breaks up, after death, are reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”
translation_text.an10.220:12.2: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
